title: Teyana Taylor Steals the Show at the 2026 Oscars
text: Teyana Taylor emerged as a standout presence at the 2026 Academy Awards, delivering memorable moments throughout the ceremony despite not winning her Best Supporting Actress nomination for One Battle After Another. When Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress, Taylor was among the first to stand and celebrate. She responded to online criticism by noting, The world holds so much misery that miserable hearts forget the face of happiness and praised genuine sportsmanship. Taylor brought a K-pop lightstick to the ceremony and enthusiastically waved it during performances. When Michael B. Jordan won Best Actor, Taylor leaped from her seat with visible joy, shouting encouragement. The two later shared an emotional embrace. Her most talked-about moment came when One Battle After Another won Best Picture when she playfully placed director Paul Thomas Anderson in a headlock while celebrating, a gesture Anderson had anticipated given their established rapport.
